purpose: Separate confirmatory scientific evidence requirements from exploratory browsing for the RED/SILSO solar-revolutions analysis.
verdict_rule: The page may call the paper causally/predictively confirmed only if every confirmatory scientific requirement is favorable. If any confirmatory requirement is unfavorable or unestimated, the public conclusion must remain exploratory/stress-test language.

confirmatory_specification.dataset: RED 1.0 included revolutionary episodes, start years 1900-2014, aligned to annualized SILSO monthly mean total sunspot number.
confirmatory_specification.primary_outcome: RED annual episode count by start year.
secondary_outcomes: RED participant proxy by start year
confirmatory_specification.primary_association_test: Pearson correlation on aligned annual global series, reported with Fisher interval and effective-sample-size caveat.
required_nulls: Permutation null for random annual overlap
required_robustness: RED measure sensitivity across episode count, participant proxy, and activity index
Transform sensitivity across raw, log1p, winsor95, and log1p+winsor95
Threshold sensitivity across any-event, top-quartile, and z-score definitions
One-control and multivariate global annual controls
Country-panel diagnostic with explicit fixed-effect identification warning
required_controls: Shiller market, inflation, and long-rate controls
JST financial crisis, public-debt, private-credit, and short-rate controls
Reinhart-Rogoff/HBS sovereign default and crisis controls
OWID conflict, food-price, regime, demographic, communications, capital-flow, and disaster controls
multiple_comparison_policy: Broad input scans must report Benjamini-Hochberg q-values.
Specification curves must report support rate, not only the strongest cherry-picked model.
Lag correlations are exploratory unless separately pre-registered and multiplicity-adjusted.
confirmatory_specification.causal_identification_rule: A country + year fixed-effects model cannot estimate a SILSO coefficient because SILSO is global and varies only by year. Therefore country-panel diagnostics cannot rescue a causal claim unless a geographically varying exposure or independent identification design is added.

causal_pathway.claim: SILSO solar activity or solar-cycle phase changes the relevant geophysical environment.
causal_pathway.required_evidence: Monthly and annual SILSO variation must show timing structure stronger than circular-shift placebo timing.

causal_pathway.claim: The global solar signal translates into country-varying radiation, geomagnetic, or auroral-proximity exposure.
causal_pathway.required_evidence: Country-specific geometry, NASA POWER radiation, magnetic-latitude, or auroral-proximity exposure must remain estimable with country and year fixed effects.

causal_pathway.claim: Exposure affects plausible mediators such as food stress, conflict, communications, institutional instability, or macroeconomic stress.
causal_pathway.required_evidence: Solar variables should predict intermediary datasets or interact with vulnerability measures in predeclared directions.

causal_pathway.claim: Exposure increases new revolutionary onset in at-risk country-years, not merely active-year counts or visual annual overlays.
causal_pathway.required_evidence: Country-year onset/event-history models must show a stable positive exposure effect after political-economy, conflict, food/agriculture, and fixed-effect controls.

causal_pathway.claim: Solar exposure adds useful signal beyond conventional predictors.
causal_pathway.required_evidence: Out-of-sample AUC, Brier score, log loss, and calibration must improve versus controls-only baselines and shifted-exposure placebo cycles.

exploratory_features.feature: Interactive RED measure, transform, threshold, and event-window controls
exploratory_features.reason: Useful for robustness browsing, but post-hoc selection would inflate evidence.

exploratory_features.feature: Solar correlation browser across all input datasets
exploratory_features.reason: Broad scans are hypothesis-generating and must be interpreted with multiple-comparison corrections.
Lag choice is not a pre-registered primary endpoint.
Current solar weather is not evidence for historical RED/SILSO association.
Approximate orbital-period aspect proxies are included for playful comparison and broad browsing, not confirmatory inference.

known_failure_modes: Solar cycle maxima recur often, so wide event windows can inflate apparent hit rates.
RED participant estimates are heavy-tailed and historically uncertain.
RED measures are mechanically related and are not independent validation.
Global annual controls cannot fully address country-specific political mechanisms.
A global solar exposure is collinear with year fixed effects in country-year panel models.
Astrological transit proxies add many low-prior exploratory comparisons and must not be treated as pre-registered mechanisms.
